clusters.title: Shutdown frees sampler snapshots while log hot paths can still hold raw pointers

member_findings.title: Shutdown frees sampler snapshots while log hot paths can still hold raw pointers
member_findings.impact: Log hot paths load `snapshot_` as a raw pointer and then dereference it without any reader lifetime guard. During shutdown, this line deletes the current snapshot immediately after an atomic exchange, so any concurrent LOG/PLOG/AccessRecorder path that already loaded the pointer can dereference freed memory and crash during worker or client teardown.
member_findings.fix: Do not reclaim snapshots until readers are quiesced. Publish disabled state before teardown and add a shutdown barrier for logging users, or replace the raw pointer scheme with a shared/RCU/hazard-pointer style lifetime so snapshots remain alive until all in-flight readers have left.

clusters.title: Avoid copying the formatted request message for every access log

member_findings.title: Avoid copying the formatted request message for every access log
member_findings.impact: Each emitted access log now calls FormatAccessReqMsg(), which returns a std::string before appending it to logStream. In the common non-sampled-marker case that helper returns reqMsg by value, adding another allocation/copy of the already formatted request payload on every foreground client/worker access-log path.
member_findings.fix: Change the helper back to an append-style API, for example AppendReqMsgWithSampleMark(std::ostream&, AccessKeyType, const std::string&), so the existing reqMsg is streamed directly and the sampled marker is appended without materializing a second full request-message string.

clusters.title: Every sampler config change retains another snapshot until shutdown

member_findings.title: Every sampler config change retains another snapshot until shutdown
member_findings.impact: Each distinct config update pushes the previous snapshot into `oldSnapshots_` and nothing frees those entries until process shutdown. A worker or client receiving repeated dynamic sampling updates will leak memory monotonically, which can eventually exhaust memory in a long-running service.
member_findings.fix: Use a bounded reclamation strategy tied to reader lifetime, such as shared snapshots or epoch/RCU reclamation, so old snapshots are freed after in-flight readers are safe instead of accumulating for the process lifetime.

clusters.title: Dynamic sampler flag transaction is untested

member_findings.title: Dynamic sampler flag transaction is untested
member_findings.impact: The new dynamic update path validates sampler flags as a batch, mutates the process gflags, rolls back on failure, and passes explicit-state bits into LogSampler. The diff only changes flags_test.cpp formatting and exercises sampler config directly, so regressions in Flags::UpdateFlagParameter, unchanged-value explicit marking, multi-flag derivation blocking, or rollback after a bad dynamic update would not be caught.
member_findings.fix: Add Flags-layer unit tests that call UpdateFlagParameter with request/access/diagnostic sample-rate maps and assert both FLAGS_* values and LogSampler snapshot ppm values. Cover request-only derivation, explicit access_sample_rate=1.0 blocking derivation, mixed sampler/non-sampler updates, and an invalid batch preserving the previous gflags and sampler snapshot.

clusters.title: Non-owning recorder setters need an explicit lifetime contract

member_findings.title: Non-owning recorder setters need an explicit lifetime contract
member_findings.impact: The new fluent facade exposes `ObjectKeyRef`, `ObjectKeysRef`, `RemoteClientId`, and similar setters that store `string_view` values or container pointers in recorder state, while the same recorder type is also moved through async/shared ownership paths. Future call sites can easily choose a `*Ref` setter for a temporary or shorter-lived object because the header does not document which setters borrow and which own, making the API hard to maintain safely as more access-log fields are added.
member_findings.fix: Document the borrow/own lifetime rule directly on the `*Ref` and `*Owned` APIs, or encode it in the API by preferring owning setters for async-capable recorders and reserving ref setters for same-scope synchronous use.
